channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Plans for a Trump family-linked resort spark protests in Albania
item.description: A coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner is facing resistance in Albania. The government says the project will transform the nation, but environmental campaigners and critics oppose it.
item.content:encoded:
A coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner is facing resistance in Albania. The government says the project will transform the nation, but environmental campaigners and critics oppose it.

(Image credit: Hameraldi Agolli)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: President Trump says he will nominate Todd Blanche to serve as attorney general
item.description: Trump said at a dinner at the White House that he plans to nominate Blanche formally, according to a video of the event posted on social media by a White House aide.
item.content:encoded:
Trump said at a dinner at the White House that he plans to nominate Blanche formally, according to a video of the event posted on social media by a White House aide.

(Image credit: Allison Robbert)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: House passes war powers resolution directing Trump to end hostilities with Iran
item.description: The vote marked a rare bipartisan rebuke of the war, but is mostly symbolic. Democrats have been unable to pass a war powers resolution in the Senate, and even if they could it would likely be vetoed.
item.content:encoded:
The vote marked a rare bipartisan rebuke of the war, but is mostly symbolic. Democrats have been unable to pass a war powers resolution in the Senate, and even if they could it would likely be vetoed.

(Image credit: Mariam Zuhaib)
item.dc:creator: Claudia Grisales

item.title: Senate Republicans start debate on ICE funding package
item.description: The Senate voted along party lines to start debate on a Republican bill to fund immigration enforcement through the end of President Trump's term.
item.content:encoded:
The Senate voted along party lines to start debate on a Republican bill to fund immigration enforcement through the end of President Trump's term.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: Trump strips job protections from 8,000 federal workers
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order that puts some 8,000 high-ranking civil servants into a new category of employees who can be fired for any reason.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump signed an executive order that puts some 8,000 high-ranking civil servants into a new category of employees who can be fired for any reason.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Trump confirms calling Netanyahu 'crazy,' says they still get along
item.description: The president also acknowledged that he cursed at the Israeli leader in a heated phone call in which he told Benjamin Netanyahu not to bomb the Lebanese capital Beirut.
item.content:encoded:
The president also acknowledged that he cursed at the Israeli leader in a heated phone call in which he told Benjamin Netanyahu not to bomb the Lebanese capital Beirut.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: People with cancer or HIV could lose Medicaid under new work rules, advocates say
item.description: Adults on Medicaid will be required to work 80 hours per month. The Trump administration says people who are sick will have to prove they are too sick to work to be exempt from the new work rules.
item.content:encoded:
Adults on Medicaid will be required to work 80 hours per month. The Trump administration says people who are sick will have to prove they are too sick to work to be exempt from the new work rules.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Michael Jackson's shadow of doubt
item.description: A sanded-down biopic about the King of pop and propaganda has resurfaced his music on the charts — along with questions about how his enduring magic became make-believe
item.content:encoded:
A sanded-down biopic about the King of pop and propaganda has resurfaced his music on the charts — along with questions about how his enduring magic became make-believe

(Image credit: Carlo Allegri/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Rodney Carmichael

item.title: Firings at CBS' '60 Minutes' reflect the fight for media control in the age of Trump
item.description: The show's new leader says he fired star Scott Pelley for insubordination. Pelley says he was defending the integrity of the show's journalism after three top executives and two reporters were fired.
item.content:encoded:
The show's new leader says he fired star Scott Pelley for insubordination. Pelley says he was defending the integrity of the show's journalism after three top executives and two reporters were fired.

(Image credit: CBS Photo Archive/CBS via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Tribeca Film Festival opens, 25 years after helping New York recover from 9/11
item.description: Actor Robert De Niro and producing partner Jane Rosenthal created the festival in the wake of the terrorist attacks on the World Trade Center.
item.content:encoded:
Actor Robert De Niro and producing partner Jane Rosenthal created the festival in the wake of the terrorist attacks on the World Trade Center.

(Image credit: Michael Ochs Archives/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco
